product.summary: The requirement contract is well-structured and covers most foundational aspects for building a public-facing conversational AI chatbot similar to ChatGPT. Core project, backend, data, security, and DevOps parameters are defined and confirmed. However, frontend, UX, and compliance implementation details remain unspecified, and the privacy policy may conflict with data sensitivity and compliance expectations.
blindspots: No defined frontend technology stack or design approach.
No explicit user experience or accessibility requirements.
No detailed compliance mapping (e.g., GDPR, CCPA) despite personal data handling.
Privacy retention policy of 'retain indefinitely' may violate compliance norms.
No mention of content moderation or abuse prevention mechanisms.
functional_gaps: Frontend interface and interaction model undefined.
Authentication and user management flow unspecified.
LLM API usage limits, caching, and fallback strategies not described.
No defined data schema or conversation storage model.
No operational or scaling strategy for high-traffic public access.
ux_considerations: Define conversational flow and tone consistency.
Provide clear feedback for errors and latency.
Include onboarding or tutorial for first-time users.
Design for mobile and desktop responsiveness.
future_phase_candidates: User account management and history retrieval.
Integration with external services (e.g., calendars, email).
product.next_focus: Define frontend architecture, UX principles, and compliance alignment before advancing to planning.

architect_reasoner.summary: The project aims to build a public-facing conversational AI chatbot similar to ChatGPT, using Python FastAPI and PostgreSQL, hosted on Microsoft Azure. The requirements are mostly complete for backend, data, security, and DevOps, but frontend, UX, and compliance details are missing. The privacy retention policy conflicts with personal data handling and compliance expectations.
architect_reasoner.feasibility: Technically feasible with moderate to high complexity. The backend stack and hosting target are appropriate for an AI system, but the full multimodal feature scope at MVP stage increases risk and resource demands. Compliance and privacy adjustments are required for feasibility under public deployment.
architect_reasoner.proposed_architecture_direction: Adopt a modular service-oriented architecture with a FastAPI backend serving REST and WebSocket endpoints, a separate frontend built with a modern JavaScript framework (React or Vue), and integration with GPT model via secure API key management. Use PostgreSQL for structured data and conversation logs, with optional caching layer (Redis) for performance. Implement strict data access controls and compliance-aware data retention.

data: PostgreSQL schema for user sessions and chat logs
Data encryption at rest and in transit
devops: CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions or Azure DevOps

architect_reasoner.data_and_api_notes: Use secure API key storage (Azure Key Vault). Implement data minimization and configurable retention to align with GDPR/CCPA. Provide structured REST endpoints for chat sessions and metadata, and WebSocket for real-time streaming. Ensure all data transfers use HTTPS and enforce authentication for any persistent user data.
architect_reasoner.infrastructure_direction: Deploy on Microsoft Azure using containerized services (Azure Container Apps or Kubernetes). Use managed PostgreSQL (Azure Database for PostgreSQL) and Redis Cache. Integrate Azure Key Vault for secrets and Azure Monitor for observability. Scale horizontally based on traffic and model API usage.
architect_reasoner.devops_direction: Implement CI/CD with automated testing, linting, and security scans. Use Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform or Bicep) for reproducible environments. Set up monitoring dashboards, alerting, and log aggregation. Include rate limiting and auto-scaling policies for public exposure.
design_principles: Data minimization and privacy by design
Resilient error handling and graceful degradation

security.summary: The project defines a public-facing conversational AI chatbot similar to ChatGPT, built with Python FastAPI and PostgreSQL, hosted on Microsoft Azure. The backend, data, and DevOps requirements are well-specified, but frontend, UX, and compliance details are missing. The privacy retention policy ('retain indefinitely') conflicts with personal data handling and compliance expectations. The architecture direction recommends a modular, secure, and compliance-aware design using modern web technologies and Azure services.
key_risks: Privacy retention policy violates data protection laws (GDPR/CCPA).
High-risk exposure due to public internet access and personal data handling.
Absence of content moderation and abuse prevention mechanisms.
Undefined frontend and UX design may lead to poor accessibility and usability.
Full multimodal feature scope at MVP stage increases technical and resource risk.
required_controls: Implement configurable data retention and deletion policies.
Add content moderation and abuse prevention filters.
Apply OAuth2/JWT authentication and rate limiting.
Use Azure Key Vault for API key and secret management.
Ensure HTTPS, encryption at rest, and secure data access controls.
Conduct regular security audits and compliance reviews.
security.privacy_notes: Personal data must be handled under strict minimization principles. Retention should be limited and configurable to comply with GDPR/CCPA. Implement user consent, data deletion requests, and anonymization for stored chat logs. Avoid indefinite retention unless justified by explicit user opt-in and legal review.
security.compliance_notes: Align with GDPR, CCPA, and other relevant data protection standards. Maintain audit trails, consent records, and transparent privacy notices. Ensure data processing agreements with third-party providers (e.g., GPT API vendor, Azure). Conduct Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) before public launch.
security.moderation_notes: Integrate automated content filtering and abuse detection. Include user reporting and escalation workflows. Prevent generation or storage of harmful, illegal, or copyrighted content. Apply rate limits and monitoring to mitigate misuse and spam.
security.incident_response_notes: Establish an incident response plan covering data breaches, API misuse, and service outages. Include detection, containment, notification, and recovery procedures. Maintain logs for forensic analysis and ensure compliance with breach notification timelines under GDPR/CCPA.

constraints.summary: The project aims to build a public-facing conversational AI chatbot similar to ChatGPT, using Python FastAPI and PostgreSQL, hosted on Microsoft Azure. The architecture direction is modular, secure, and compliance-aware. Backend, data, and DevOps requirements are well-defined, but frontend, UX, and compliance implementation details remain incomplete. Privacy retention policy conflicts with compliance norms and must be revised before planning. The system will require strong authentication, moderation, and data protection controls to mitigate high-risk exposure.
constraints.cost_range: USD 250,000–600,000 (initial MVP with full multimodal capability and Azure hosting)
constraints.maintainability_notes: Adopt modular service boundaries (frontend, backend, data, security). Use Infrastructure-as-Code and CI/CD pipelines for reproducibility. Maintain strict version control and automated testing. Implement observability and structured logging for maintainability. Ensure documentation of API contracts and data schemas.

phased_delivery.phase_1: Define frontend architecture, UX principles, and compliance alignment. Implement core FastAPI backend and GPT API integration.
phased_delivery.phase_2: Add authentication, rate limiting, and moderation modules. Deploy MVP with secure data storage and monitoring.
phased_delivery.phase_3: Enhance scalability, add personalization, multilingual support, and advanced analytics. Conduct compliance audits and performance optimization.

tradeoffs.scope_vs_timeline: Full multimodal feature set at MVP increases cost and delays; consider phased rollout.
tradeoffs.privacy_vs_personalization: Data minimization may limit personalization features; configurable retention can balance compliance and user experience.
tradeoffs.performance_vs_cost: High concurrency and LLM API usage may require caching and scaling strategies that increase infrastructure cost.
tradeoffs.security_vs_usability: Strict authentication and moderation may slightly reduce user convenience but are essential for public deployment.

implementation_pressure_points: Compliance alignment with GDPR/CCPA due to personal data handling.
Content moderation and abuse prevention for public exposure.
Scalability and cost management for multimodal LLM integration.
Frontend UX and accessibility design for general public use.
Secure API key management and data encryption across Azure services.

critic.summary: The requirement contract for the public conversational AI chatbot is mostly complete and technically feasible. Backend, data, security, and DevOps parameters are well-defined, but frontend, UX, and compliance implementation details remain missing. The privacy retention policy ('retain indefinitely') conflicts with personal data handling and compliance expectations. The architecture direction is sound—modular, secure, and Azure-based—but compliance and moderation controls must be addressed before planning.
contradictions: Privacy retention policy of 'retain indefinitely' conflicts with personal data sensitivity and compliance standards (GDPR/CCPA).
MVP scope includes 'all ChatGPT features' while constraints emphasize limited resources, creating a feasibility tension.
blindspots: No defined frontend technology stack or UX design principles.
No explicit accessibility or usability requirements.
No detailed compliance mapping or DPIA process.
No content moderation or abuse prevention mechanisms.
No authentication or user management flow defined.

unresolved_questions.frontend_stack: Which frontend framework (e.g., React, Vue, Angular) will be used for the conversational UI?
unresolved_questions.ux_guidelines: What are the UX and accessibility standards to be followed (e.g., WCAG compliance)?
unresolved_questions.data_retention: Should data retention be configurable or time-limited to meet compliance requirements?
unresolved_questions.moderation_policy: What level of content moderation and abuse prevention is required for public deployment?
unresolved_questions.compliance_scope: Which specific compliance frameworks (GDPR, CCPA, etc.) must the system adhere to?

corrective_actions: Revise privacy retention policy to support configurable or time-limited retention aligned with GDPR/CCPA.
Define frontend architecture and UX principles, including accessibility and responsiveness.
Add compliance alignment details and plan for a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA).
Specify authentication, authorization, and user management flows.
Integrate content moderation and abuse prevention mechanisms.
Reassess MVP scope to align with resource constraints and phased delivery.
priority_order: Resolve privacy retention and compliance contradictions.
Establish moderation and abuse prevention strategy.
Clarify authentication and user management design.
Reevaluate MVP scope versus resource constraints.

core_services.name: gateway_service
core_services.description: Handles incoming HTTP and WebSocket requests, routing to appropriate backend modules. Implements rate limiting, authentication, and request validation.

core_services.name: conversation_service
core_services.description: Manages chat sessions, message persistence, and conversation context. Interfaces with LLM integration for generating responses.

core_services.name: llm_proxy_service
core_services.description: Encapsulates GPT API calls, manages API key rotation, caching, and fallback logic. Provides unified interface for text and multimodal queries.

core_services.name: user_service
core_services.description: Handles user authentication, session management, and optional profile data. Supports OAuth2/JWT tokens.

core_services.name: moderation_service
core_services.description: Performs content filtering, abuse detection, and logging of flagged interactions for review.

schema_overview.users: Stores user credentials, tokens, and preferences.
schema_overview.sessions: Tracks active chat sessions and metadata.
schema_overview.messages: Stores user and AI messages with timestamps and moderation flags.

service_design.deployment_model: Containerized microservices deployed on Azure Container Apps or AKS with managed PostgreSQL and Redis.

endpoints.description: Send a message and receive AI response.
Authenticate user and issue JWT token.
Perform content moderation on text or media.

security.authorization: Role-based access control for admin and moderation endpoints

api_patterns.rate_limiting: Per-IP and per-user limits enforced at gateway level

business_modules.conversation_management: Handles session lifecycle, message persistence, and context tracking.
business_modules.user_authentication: Implements OAuth2/JWT-based login and token refresh.
business_modules.moderation_and_safety: Filters harmful or non-compliant content using rule-based and ML-assisted checks.
business_modules.analytics_and_audit: Captures usage metrics, error rates, and compliance audit logs.
business_modules.admin_dashboard: Provides internal tools for monitoring sessions, flagged content, and system health.

llm_integration_design.provider: OpenAI GPT model via API key
llm_integration_design.access_method: Secure HTTPS API calls through llm_proxy_service
llm_integration_design.key_management: Azure Key Vault for API key storage and rotation

response_handling.fallback: Graceful degradation to cached or templated responses on API failure

llm_integration_design.moderation_hook: All LLM outputs pass through moderation_service before delivery
llm_integration_design.observability: Log latency, token usage, and error rates for each LLM call

background_jobs.scheduler: Celery or Azure Functions Timer Triggers
background_jobs.monitoring: Job success/failure metrics integrated with Azure Monitor

failure_handling.retry_policies: Exponential backoff for transient API or DB errors
failure_handling.circuit_breakers: Protect LLM API from overload or cascading failures
failure_handling.graceful_degradation: Fallback to cached responses or user-friendly error messages
failure_handling.data_integrity: Transactional writes with rollback on failure
failure_handling.incident_response: Automated alerts and manual escalation workflow for critical failures

scaling_notes.strategy: Horizontal scaling of stateless services; vertical scaling for DB as needed
scaling_notes.autoscaling: Azure autoscale rules based on CPU, memory, and request latency
scaling_notes.caching: Redis caching for frequent prompts and session metadata
scaling_notes.load_balancing: Azure Front Door or Application Gateway for global traffic distribution
scaling_notes.observability: Azure Application Insights dashboards for latency, throughput, and error tracking

backend_risks.privacy_compliance: Indefinite data retention conflicts with GDPR/CCPA; must implement configurable retention.
backend_risks.cost_risk: High LLM API usage may exceed budget; caching and usage throttling required.
backend_risks.scalability_risk: Full multimodal support at MVP may strain infrastructure; phased rollout recommended.
backend_risks.security_risk: Public exposure increases attack surface; strict authentication and monitoring required.
backend_risks.moderation_risk: Inadequate filtering could lead to harmful content exposure; continuous tuning needed.

auth_design.authentication_method: OAuth2 with JWT tokens for session management
auth_design.identity_provider: Azure Active Directory B2C or equivalent public identity service
auth_design.api_authentication: Bearer tokens with short-lived access and refresh tokens
auth_design.session_security: Use HttpOnly, Secure cookies with SameSite=strict for web sessions
auth_design.multi_factor_authentication: Optional for registered users; enforced for admin and moderation accounts
auth_design.account_protection: Brute-force detection, CAPTCHA on repeated failed logins, and IP-based throttling

roles.public_user: Limited access to chatbot interface and public endpoints
roles.registered_user: Access to personalized chat history and settings
roles.moderator: Access to content review and abuse reports
roles.admin: Full system management and audit access

authorization_model.policy_enforcement: Authorization middleware in FastAPI using dependency injection
authorization_model.data_access_controls: Row-level security in PostgreSQL for user-specific data isolation
authorization_model.least_privilege_principle: All roles and services operate with minimal required permissions

secrets_management.storage: Azure Key Vault for all API keys, database credentials, and encryption keys
secrets_management.rotation_policy: Automatic rotation every 90 days or upon incident
secrets_management.access_control: Managed identities for services; no hardcoded secrets in code or configs
secrets_management.encryption: AES-256 for stored secrets; TLS for retrieval
secrets_management.audit: Access logs for all secret retrievals stored in centralized audit system

abuse_prevention.rate_limiting: Per-IP and per-user rate limits using FastAPI middleware and Redis counters
abuse_prevention.content_moderation: Automated LLM-based and rule-based filters for harmful or illegal content
abuse_prevention.user_reporting: In-app reporting mechanism with escalation to moderators
abuse_prevention.spam_and_bot_protection: reCAPTCHA or hCaptcha for anonymous users and signup flows
abuse_prevention.api_abuse_detection: Anomaly detection on API usage patterns via Azure Monitor and custom alerts
abuse_prevention.output_filtering: Post-processing filters to remove disallowed or sensitive outputs

privacy_controls.data_minimization: Store only necessary metadata and anonymized chat logs where possible
privacy_controls.retention_policy: Configurable retention (default 90 days) with user opt-in for longer storage
privacy_controls.user_rights: Support for data export, correction, and deletion requests (GDPR/CCPA compliance)
privacy_controls.consent_management: Explicit consent for data collection and retention at first use
privacy_controls.encryption: AES-256 encryption at rest (PostgreSQL TDE) and TLS 1.3 in transit
privacy_controls.anonymization: Hash or pseudonymize user identifiers in stored logs
privacy_controls.third_party_disclosure: Transparent disclosure of GPT API and Azure as subprocessors

audit_and_logging_controls.log_scope: Authentication events, API access, data changes, moderation actions, and system errors
audit_and_logging_controls.log_storage: Centralized logging via Azure Monitor and Application Insights
audit_and_logging_controls.retention: Logs retained for 180 days with secure archival
audit_and_logging_controls.integrity_protection: Immutable log storage with cryptographic signing for critical events
audit_and_logging_controls.access_control: Restricted access to logs based on RBAC roles
audit_and_logging_controls.alerting: Real-time alerts for suspicious activity, failed logins, and data access anomalies
audit_and_logging_controls.compliance_audit: Quarterly internal security and compliance audits

plan_components: Detection and triage of security or privacy incidents
User and authority notification within legal timelines (GDPR/CCPA)
incident_response_notes.communication_protocol: Dedicated incident response channel with escalation to security lead
incident_response_notes.backup_and_recovery: Daily encrypted backups with tested restoration procedures
incident_response_notes.testing: Annual tabletop exercises and simulated breach drills

high: Public exposure with personal data handling increases breach risk
Indefinite retention policy conflicts with compliance requirements
Potential misuse of chatbot for harmful or illegal content generation
medium: API key leakage risk if not properly isolated
Denial-of-service attacks due to open public access
Insufficient moderation could lead to reputational damage
security_risks.mitigation_summary: Apply configurable retention, strict access control, automated moderation, and continuous monitoring to reduce exposure and ensure compliance.

User.notes: Stores minimal personal identifiers; authentication handled via OAuth2/JWT.

Conversation.notes: Links user sessions to chat logs; metadata includes device, locale, and session context.

Message.notes: Stores individual chat messages; content subject to moderation and anonymization.

LLMRequestLog.notes: Captures GPT API usage metrics for cost tracking and performance analytics.

AuditEvent.notes: Used for compliance and security auditing; includes data access and deletion events.

storage_design.primary_db: PostgreSQL (Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server)
storage_design.caching_layer: Redis (Azure Cache for Redis) for session and token caching
storage_design.encryption: AES-256 encryption at rest; TLS 1.2+ for data in transit
storage_design.indexing: Indexes on user_id, conversation_id, and timestamp for efficient retrieval
storage_design.backup_strategy: Daily encrypted backups with 30-day retention; stored in Azure Blob Storage

schema_notes.normalization: 3NF for core entities; denormalized views for analytics queries
schema_notes.privacy_controls: PII stored separately from message content; user_id pseudonymized in analytics tables
schema_notes.moderation_flags: Messages include moderation_status field for filtering and review
schema_notes.audit_trail: All data access and modification events logged in AuditEvent table

retention_and_deletion.policy: Configurable retention period (default 12 months); indefinite retention only with explicit user opt-in
retention_and_deletion.deletion_process: Soft delete followed by scheduled hard purge; anonymization for compliance requests
retention_and_deletion.user_controls: Users can request data deletion or export via privacy dashboard

consistency_notes.transaction_model: ACID transactions for PostgreSQL writes; eventual consistency for analytics aggregation
consistency_notes.cache_sync: Redis cache invalidated on message or conversation updates
consistency_notes.data_integrity: Foreign key constraints enforced; periodic integrity checks via scheduled jobs

migration_notes.initial_setup: Schema migration via Alembic; version-controlled migrations in CI/CD pipeline
migration_notes.future_expansion: Support for additional tables (e.g., user preferences, moderation logs)
migration_notes.data_import: Legacy data import scripts must anonymize PII before ingestion
migration_notes.rollback_strategy: Transactional rollback supported; backups used for disaster recovery

data_risks.privacy_risk: Personal data exposure if indefinite retention persists; mitigated by configurable retention and anonymization
data_risks.security_risk: Public internet exposure increases attack surface; mitigated by HTTPS, authentication, and rate limiting
data_risks.compliance_risk: Non-aligned retention policy may violate GDPR/CCPA; requires DPIA and legal review
data_risks.operational_risk: High concurrency and LLM API usage may cause performance degradation; mitigated by caching and scaling
data_risks.moderation_risk: Potential storage of harmful or copyrighted content; mitigated by automated filtering and audit logging

deployment_topology.architecture: Modular microservice-style deployment on Microsoft Azure

components.frontend: Static web app (React) hosted via Azure Front Door + CDN
components.backend: Python FastAPI container deployed on Azure App Service or Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
components.cache: Azure Cache for Redis for session and response caching
components.secrets: Azure Key Vault for API keys and credentials
components.storage: Azure Blob Storage for logs, media, and backups

networking.ingress: Azure Front Door with WAF and DDoS protection
networking.communication: HTTPS-only, internal service-to-service via private endpoints
networking.scaling: Horizontal auto-scaling based on CPU, memory, and request latency

dev.deployment: Ephemeral environments via CI/CD preview builds

staging.deployment: Mirrors production topology with reduced scale
staging.data: Limited real data under strict access control

prod.deployment: Fully scaled, monitored, and secured environment
prod.data: Personal and conversational data under compliance controls

ci_cd_design.pipeline_tooling: GitHub Actions integrated with Azure DevOps
stages: Blue-green or canary deployment to production
ci_cd_design.secrets_management: All secrets pulled from Azure Key Vault at runtime
ci_cd_design.artifact_storage: Azure Container Registry for images, Azure Artifacts for dependencies

observability_stack.logging: Structured JSON logs via Azure Monitor and Application Insights
observability_stack.metrics: Request latency, error rates, throughput, and LLM API usage metrics
observability_stack.tracing: Distributed tracing with OpenTelemetry integrated into FastAPI
observability_stack.alerting: Azure Monitor alerts for uptime, latency, and error thresholds
observability_stack.dashboards: Custom dashboards for performance, cost, and compliance KPIs
observability_stack.audit_trail: Immutable audit logs for data access and administrative actions

rollback_strategy.deployment_model: Blue-green with traffic switching via Azure Front Door
rollback_strategy.rollback_process: Automated reversion to previous container image and configuration snapshot
rollback_strategy.data_consistency: Database migrations versioned and reversible using Alembic

backup_and_recovery.database_backups: Automated daily backups with 30-day retention via Azure Database for PostgreSQL
backup_and_recovery.key_vault: Periodic export of encrypted secrets metadata
backup_and_recovery.disaster_recovery: Cross-region replication and failover to secondary Azure region

cost_controls.monitoring: Azure Cost Management and Budgets with alert thresholds
cost_controls.scaling_policies: Auto-scale rules with upper limits to prevent runaway costs
cost_controls.resource_tagging: Environment and project-based tagging for cost attribution
cost_controls.reserved_instances: Use reserved capacity for PostgreSQL and compute after baseline load established
cost_controls.LLM_API_usage: Rate limiting and caching to reduce API call costs

high: Compliance misalignment due to indefinite data retention
Public exposure increases attack surface and abuse potential
Full multimodal feature set at MVP may exceed resource capacity
medium: Complex CI/CD pipeline increases maintenance overhead
Scaling costs for LLM API usage may fluctuate unpredictably
Dependency on third-party GPT API introduces external SLA risk
mitigations: Implement configurable data retention and anonymization
Add content moderation and abuse detection filters
Use caching and request batching for LLM API efficiency
Conduct regular security and compliance audits
Adopt phased rollout to manage feature and cost growth
